feature_name: yt-dlp module MeTube parity (v1.12.0): one-shot download, dropdowns, per-channel N, pause/edit, poll-status, + 3 confirmed bug fixes
git_ownership_note: Coordinator owns ALL git. SDEs must NOT commit/stage/push. SDE reports files-changed + test output; coordinator commits per task. Node 22 is the test/verify standard (coordinator has a Node 22 toolchain; SDEs verify on Node 22, not just 24).
supersedes: feature/ytdlp-oneshot-download -- the parked one-shot-download discovery is FOLDED IN as workstream A; this feature absorbs it.
builds_on: The shipped optional yt-dlp module (v1.11.0 / v1.11.1): lib/ytdlp/{config,index,store,url,args,run,process}.js + the dedicated /subscriptions page. This feature EXTENDS that module; it does NOT re-litigate its architecture or its locked decisions D1-D5.
one_feature_note: Dean's directive: ONE cohesive feature (9 workstreams A-I), held to MeTube's SIMPLE shape (a form + a list with per-item status). NOT a job-queue cathedral. Prefer reusing existing lib/ytdlp primitives over new machinery. Do NOT gold-plate.
hard_constraint: OPTIONAL / ADDITIVE / MUST-NOT-DEGRADE. When the module is DISABLED (default, FILETUBE_YTDLP_ENABLED off) FileTube stays BYTE-IDENTICAL: no new routes (incl. the new one-shot download + status + edit endpoints), no background poll, no subscriptions/one-shot UI. The disabled-path-is-a-no-op guarantee remains the acceptance north star and MUST keep an explicit test. EXCEPTION-BY-DESIGN: bug fix F (clean display titles) and G-hardening (realpath/resolve scan-root normalization) touch CORE scan/title code that runs for ALL users -- those MUST be scoped so non-yt-dlp library files are NOT rewritten and behavior for existing non-yt-dlp trees is unchanged. Bug fix I (deleted-stays-gone) is CONFIRMED already working = no code change, AC-only.

confirmed_carryover_constraints.security_core: REUSE the T3/T4 security core for the NEW one-shot endpoint (spawns yt-dlp on a user-supplied URL = T3/T4-class): validateChannelUrl (allowlist+normalize, userinfo-reject, decoded-id charset), arg-array via execFile (NEVER shell:true), `--` positional separator before the URL, path confinement on the folder param (resolveChannelDir / startsWith(root+path.sep)), cookies redaction on EVERY sink (log + returned error + persisted field), timeout + SIGKILL, --restrict-filenames (do NOT remove -- SF4).
confirmed_carryover_constraints.E1_mount_loss: KEEP the E1 OR-gate intact: extraScanRoots returns the download dir when isEnabled(config) OR fs.existsSync(downloadDir). The display-only folder auto-registration (G) must NOT reintroduce the C3/C7/D2 disable-reap/eviction surface and must NOT make any scan/prune path depend on synthetic db.folders presence.
confirmed_carryover_constraints.D3_delete: Delete in FileTube = STAYS GONE; .ytdlp-archive.txt persists through UI delete + prune-missing; subscription-delete deliberately does not touch the archive.
confirmed_carryover_constraints.node: Node 22 LTS is the standard (.nvmrc / .node-version = 22). Verify tests on Node 22, not just 24 (a Node-22-only .unref() timeout hang bit us before). CI is green-gated on Node 22.
confirmed_carryover_constraints.poll_status_transport: POLLING confirmed by Dean (UI polls ~2-3s). NO WebSocket.
confirmed_carryover_constraints.npm_env_note: Every node/npm command in this repo needs `export PATH="/home/coder/.local/share/fnm/node-versions/v24.14.0/installation/bin:$PATH"` first (fnm not auto-sourced). Build stage additionally runs on Node 22 per CI/.nvmrc.

workstreams.A: One-shot URL download: POST /api/ytdlp/download INSIDE the isEnabled gate. Single-video URL -> downloads once into a folder, no persistent subscription. LOCKED (from the parked discovery): serialize with the poll loop (no parallel spawns); single-video only (reject channel/playlist with 400); default subfolder 'One-Off'; 202 + background, fire-and-forget. Reuse ALL T3/T4 security. Small UI: paste URL + the dropdowns from B.
workstreams.B: Format + quality DROPDOWNS on BOTH the subscription form AND the one-shot form. Media type = audio/video; quality = existing normalizeQuality allowlist values (default best). Replace any free-text with dropdowns. MeTube-simple.
workstreams.C: Per-channel 'download last N': per-subscription override of global FILETUBE_YTDLP_MAX_VIDEOS (default 25). Settable in add/edit form. Applied as --playlist-end N for that sub's list pass; global default when unset.
workstreams.D: Pause/resume + EDIT a subscription: an edit endpoint (PATCH /api/subscriptions/:id or similar) to change format/quality/N without delete-readd, and a `paused` flag (paused subs skipped by the poll loop; UI toggle). Add/edit via the same form.
workstreams.E: Live status via POLLING: replace the static 'Pending...' with real progress. Parse yt-dlp download progress (% / eta on stdout/stderr) into per-subscription/per-download status (state: queued/listing/downloading/done/error, current video title, N of M, percent). Expose via a status field on GET /api/subscriptions (or dedicated GET /api/subscriptions/status) polled ~2-3s. Keep it simple: in-memory current-activity + persisted lastStatus. MeTube UX shape (pending -> downloading % -> finished/error per item).
workstreams.F: BUG (clean display titles) -- DISPLAY-ONLY. Title is currently path.basename(info.name, info.ext) at server.js:998, which for downloads is the --restrict-filenames name 'Title_With_Underscores [<id>].ext'. Fix via a tightly-scoped helper: strip a trailing ' [<11-char youtube id>]' and convert '_'->space (regex ~ /^(.*?)[ _]\[[A-Za-z0-9_-]{11}\]$/) at title derivation. DO NOT remove --restrict-filenames (SF4). [id] CONFIRMED non-load-bearing (getMediaId hashes the PATH at server.js:511; dedup uses the separate .ytdlp-archive.txt). Works for existing files, no id churn, no db migration. Scope the regex TIGHTLY so non-ytdlp library files aren't rewritten.
workstreams.G: BUG #6 + FEATURE #7 (unified fix): duplicate entries + auto-register the download folder as a managed playlist. ROOT CAUSE of duplicates: media id = md5(absolute path) (server.js:511) + currentFolders = Set([...db.folders, ...extraScanRoots]) (server.js:955) dedups only BYTE-IDENTICAL root strings; db.folders stored as-typed/unresolved (~server.js:1258-1264) while extraScanRoots returns path.resolve(downloadDir) -> a bind-mount/symlink/relative spelling of the same tree is walked twice -> two path-based ids -> two rows. FIX = two parts: (1) HARDENING: realpath/normalize the merged scan roots before the Set dedup (realpath-per-ROOT is cheap; per-file too costly) + path.resolve db.folders on write, so divergent spellings collapse. (2) DISPLAY-ONLY MERGE (Dean-approved; do NOT persist into db.folders): GET /api/config + sidebar/playlists UI include the module's extraScanRoots as a SYNTHETIC folder WITHOUT writing db.folders. extraScanRoots stays the AUTHORITATIVE scan root + mount-loss protection (E1 intact); db presence is pure UI affordance. Renamable via persisted folderSettings[downloadDir].name (persist ONLY folderSettings, not the folder). Self-heals on launch (derived from extraScanRoots each time). Removes the need for a manual add (kills the dup bug) WITHOUT the C3/C7/D2 disable-reap/eviction surface. NOTE: softens locked decision C7(ii) ('config never lists a folder the operator didn't add') -- INTENDED + Dean-approved; PE documents the reconciliation and confirms NO scan/prune path depends on the synthetic db.folders presence.
workstreams.H: Embed metadata + thumbnails: add --embed-metadata (and --embed-thumbnail where supported) to download args for audio AND video (MeTube embeds metadata for audio only; Dean wants it explicit for both). Confirm the yt-dlp flags + postprocessor deps are available in the pinned image (ffmpeg is present). Also makes tags.title available (complements F for future files).
workstreams.I: Deleted-stays-gone: CONFIRMED already working (.ytdlp-archive.txt persists through UI delete + prune-missing; next poll skips archived ids; subscription-delete deliberately doesn't touch the archive per D3). NO code change. AC-only: assert the guarantee + docs note on the archive-persistence dependency (esp. network-share download dirs: if the share/archive is unavailable at poll time, dedup is lost and the channel re-downloads).

confirmed_mechanics_do_not_reinvestigate: F: title source = path.basename(info.name, info.ext) at server.js:998 (EM-VERIFIED against current code).

G: media id = crypto.createHash('md5').update(filePath).digest('hex') at server.js:511 (EM-VERIFIED); currentFolders = Array.from(new Set([...(db.folders||[]), ...ytdlp.extraScanRoots(ytdlpConfig)])) at server.js:955 (EM-VERIFIED; byte-identical-string dedup only); db.folders entries stored as-typed/unresolved ~server.js:1258-1264.

F: getMediaId hashes the PATH not the [id]; dedup uses the separate .ytdlp-archive.txt -> the [id] in the filename is non-load-bearing for identity/dedup, so display-only title cleanup is safe (no id churn, no migration).
I: archive persistence through UI delete + prune-missing is already implemented (D3, shipped in v1.11.0).
process_plan: Full pipeline. Discovery (PM: 9 workstreams -> grouped ACs; F/G/I bug mechanics are CONFIRMED -> write as concrete pass/fail ACs, not open investigations). Design (PE: REAL design needed -- the polling status subsystem, edit/pause + CRUD/edit endpoint shapes, the display-only folder merge + realpath hardening, the UI forms/dropdowns, the metadata flags). Implementation (likely MULTIPLE SDE tasks: server endpoints, UI, args/metadata, title/dedup fixes). Build (Node 22). TWO-REVIEWER GATE (the one-shot endpoint spawns yt-dlp on a user URL = T3/T4-class; the status-parser + folder-merge also warrant review). Acceptance (PM: explicit per-criterion pass/fail). Ship: merge -> tag v1.12.0 -> Docker publish (CI is the image arbiter) -> Dean's on-device pass.
two_reviewer_gate_scope: The one-shot POST /api/ytdlp/download endpoint (user-URL yt-dlp spawn, T3/T4 class), the status-progress parser, and the folder display-only merge + realpath hardening.
product_manager_discovery_summary: Discovery complete. Wrote docs/exec-plans/active/2026-07-06-ytdlp-metube-parity.md with Goal/Scope/Out-of-scope/Constraints (leading with OPTIONAL-ADDITIVE-NO-DEGRADE + the F/G core-scan exception), FR-A..FR-I functional requirements, NFR1-4 security/reliability requirements, testability requirements, and 60 numbered+tagged acceptance criteria grouped by: disabled-no-op (7), A one-shot (6), B dropdowns (4), C per-channel N (3), D pause/edit (6), E live status (6), F title cleanup incl. 2 regression ACs (5), G dedup+folder-merge incl. 1 distinct-trees regression AC (10), H embed metadata (4), I deleted-stays-gone (2), cross-cutting security reuse (5), cross-cutting Node22/process (2). No new product forks surfaced -- all locks from the inbox/bootstrap carried through unchanged (confirmed explicitly in the plan's Open Questions section as 'None'). Verified against current code (server.js:511/:955/:998, lib/ytdlp/args.js, store.js, config.js, index.js) that F/D/E/G's gaps are real (no edit endpoint, no paused flag, no per-sub maxVideos field, no one-shot endpoint, no status/progress parser, quality is currently free-text on the subscription form, GET /api/config does not yet surface extraScanRoots) -- so the ACs target genuine, currently-missing behavior, not already-shipped functionality.

tasks.title: Lib primitives (pure, no routes): per-sub maxVideos, embed flags, classifySingleVideo, updateSubscription + paused/maxVideos validation & backfill
tasks.description: The pure, low-risk foundation everything downstream builds on. Per the exec-plan ## Design (FR-C, FR-H, FR-A url, FR-D store). (1) lib/ytdlp/args.js: in buildYtdlpListArgs compute the effective per-sub bound inline with precedence sub.maxVideos ?? config.maxVideos (config already defaults to 25; 0 = unlimited) and feed the EXISTING playlistEndArgs (signature unchanged, already reads .maxVideos). In buildYtdlpDownloadArgs add --embed-metadata AND --embed-thumbnail (each its own argv element) to BOTH the audio and video branches; keep --restrict-filenames + the -- discipline unchanged. (2) lib/ytdlp/url.js: add classifySingleVideo(raw) = run validateChannelUrl FIRST (all existing security checks), then inspect the normalized URL: youtu.be/<id> and youtube.com/watch?v=<id> -> {ok:true, videoId}; anything channel/playlist/@handle/c/user -> {ok:false, error}; re-check videoId with isSafeVideoId. SINGLE source of truth -- do NOT fork validateChannelUrl. (3) lib/ytdlp/store.js: add updateSubscription(deps, id, patch) via updateDatabase that mutates ONLY provided fields, preserving id/channelUrl/name/addedAt/lastCheckedAt/lastStatus/archive, returns updated record or null; add maxVideos (integer [0, MAX_SUB_MAX_VIDEOS ~5000], else invalid) + paused (boolean, default false) validation in validateSubscriptionInput and a per-sub backfill in ensureYtdlp (if typeof sub.paused !== 'boolean' => false). NO new routes, NO orchestration, NO UI in T1 -- pure lib + validators only. Keep every file require()-safe.
tasks.done_when: buildYtdlpListArgs honors per-sub maxVideos over the global (AC18) with global fallback unregressed (AC19) and bounds rejection wired (AC20 at the validator level); buildYtdlpDownloadArgs includes --embed-metadata for audio (AC48) + video (AC49) and --embed-thumbnail for both (AC50); classifySingleVideo accepts single-video, rejects channel/playlist/handle, reuses validateChannelUrl (AC54 foundation); updateSubscription preserves untouched fields (AC21 foundation) + returns null for unknown id (AC24 foundation); paused/maxVideos validation + ensureYtdlp backfill land. [UNIT] tests for C/H/D-validation/classify. Lint 0; full existing suite green; disabled path byte-identical (no route/orchestration touched). Verify on Node 22.

tasks.title: FR-E progress parser + activity map + onProgress threading (TWO-REVIEWER GATE)
tasks.description: Per exec-plan ## Design (FR-E). (1) NEW lib/ytdlp/progress.js: pure parseProgressLine(line) -> patch|null recognizing yt-dlp --newline shapes ([download] NN.N% -> {percent}; Downloading item N of M -> {index,total}; Destination: .../Title [id].ext -> {title: cleanDisplayTitle(basename)}; 100% / 'has already been downloaded' -> {percent:100}); null for non-progress lines; never touches process state, never sees cookies. (2) NEW lib/ytdlp/activity.js: ephemeral in-process, NON-persisted map {subscriptions:{}, oneShots:{}} with setSubscription/setOneShot (shallow-merge + stamp updatedAt), clearSubscription, getSnapshot (plain-object copy; prune terminal one-shots older than ONESHOT_TTL_MS=5min), resetForTests. LiveEntry shape per design; for state 'error' store ONLY the redacted safeErrorStatus string -- NEVER raw error/stderr (NFR3/AC31). (3) lib/ytdlp/run.js: thread opts={onProgress} 4th arg into runDownload -> spawnYtdlpDownload; on the DOWNLOAD path ONLY: add --newline (in args), change download stdio to ['ignore','pipe','pipe'], attach a chunk-boundary-safe (StringDecoder) line-splitter to BOTH stdout and stderr, parse each complete line -> onProgress(patch) for non-null; stdout is PARSED-AND-DISCARDED (never accumulated); partial-last-line carry capped; keep the SF3 bounded stderr diagnostic tail unchanged; add the SF7 stdout.on('error') guard mirroring the list path. List path UNTOUCHED. MUST preserve SF1 redaction (onProgress never receives argv), SF3 (no maxBuffer/no accumulation), SF7 (settled-guard/no double-resolve/no hang). Unit tests: parser fixtures (AC27), activity map get/set/prune/redaction (AC31). FLAGGED for the full two-reviewer gate (streaming/redaction invariant preservation).
tasks.done_when: parseProgressLine extracts state/title/index/total/percent from captured fixtures + returns null for non-progress (AC27); activity map set/merge/getSnapshot/prune/reset work and an error entry carries NO cookies path (AC31); runDownload accepts onProgress and forwards download stdout/stderr line-by-line without accumulation, --newline added download-only, SF1/SF3/SF7 invariants provably intact (no maxBuffer, redaction upstream, stdout error guard). Lint 0; full suite green; disabled no-op intact. Node 22. Then the two-reviewer gate (QA agent + separate /code-review) on the streaming/redaction changes.

tasks.title: Server endpoints + orchestration in index.js: runExclusive, runOneShot, POST /api/ytdlp/download, PATCH /api/subscriptions/:id, GET /api/subscriptions/status, activity wiring (TWO-REVIEWER GATE)
tasks.description: Per exec-plan ## Design (FR-A serialization+endpoint, FR-D endpoint, FR-E exposure). All routes registered INSIDE the isEnabled gate (disabled => native 404, AC1-3). (1) runExclusive FIFO gate (ytdlpTail promise chain) so the poll body AND one-shot NEVER spawn yt-dlp concurrently; keep pollBusy + coalesce UNCHANGED (distinct job: they prevent stacking redundant polls; runExclusive serializes spawns across poll+one-shot). (2) POST /api/ytdlp/download: sync-validate url via classifySingleVideo (channel/playlist/malformed => 400, NEVER spawns AC9/10), format via VALID_FORMATS (default video), quality via normalizeQuality (default best AC16/17), folder (default 'One-Off') confined via resolveChannelDir (AC55); respond 202 {accepted,jobId=randomUUID}; background runOneShot via runExclusive with its OWN try/catch, NO db lock across the await. runOneShot: activity.setOneShot downloading -> run.runDownload(syntheticSub,config,[videoId],{onProgress}) reusing --restrict-filenames/--download-archive/timeout+SIGKILL/cookies-redaction verbatim (NFR1/AC54-58) -> quarantineEscapedDownloads BEFORE scanDirectories (SF4) -> done/error(safeErrorStatus). (3) PATCH /api/subscriptions/:id: subset of {format,quality,maxVideos,paused}; validate (format=>400, quality neutralized via normalizeQuality, maxVideos integer-bound=>400, paused boolean=>400); store.updateSubscription; 404 unknown (AC21/24). (4) GET /api/subscriptions/status -> activity.getSnapshot() (AC28/30/32). (5) Pause semantics: runPoll filters out paused subs ONLY for the all-subs case (scheduled + re-pull-all, AC22/23); a specific-subId re-pull runs even if paused (manual override allowed, no 409). (6) activity wiring in runPoll/runSubscriptionCycle/processSubscription (queued/listing/downloading/done/error transitions per design); clearSubscription on subscription delete. Integration tests for A/D/E-exposure + serialization spy (AC12) + disabled-no-op. FLAGGED for the full two-reviewer gate (the one-shot user-URL yt-dlp spawn = T3/T4-class security).
tasks.done_when: POST /api/ytdlp/download 202+background on single-video (AC8), 400 no-spawn on channel/playlist/malformed (AC9/10), lands in One-Off + indexed (AC11), serialized-with-poll proven by an invocation-boundary spy (AC12); PATCH changes fields preserving addedAt/lastCheckedAt/lastStatus/archive (AC21), 404 unknown (AC24), paused skipped by scheduled+re-pull-all (AC22) and unpause resumes (AC23); GET /api/subscriptions/status reflects in-flight state distinct from lastStatus (AC28) + terminal states (AC30); all new routes 404 when disabled (AC1/2/3); security reuse proven (arg-array + -- with metachar URL AC56, folder confinement AC55, cookies redaction on new sinks AC57, timeout+SIGKILL AC58, no-lock-across-download). Lint 0; full suite green; Node 22. Then the full two-reviewer gate.

tasks.title: FR-F + FR-G core server.js changes: cleanDisplayTitle, normalizeScanRoot + scan-root merge, path.resolve on config write, display-only synthetic folder (TWO-REVIEWER GATE)
tasks.description: Per exec-plan ## Design (FR-F, FR-G). These touch CORE always-on server.js (NOT behind isEnabled) -- scope tightly + prove non-yt-dlp behavior unchanged. (1) cleanDisplayTitle(baseName) helper (exported like getMediaId for unit test): regex /^(.*?)[ _]\[[A-Za-z0-9_-]{11}\]$/ -> strip trailing bracketed EXACTLY-11-char id + underscore->space; no match => return baseName unchanged. Apply at server.js:998 title derivation: cleanDisplayTitle(path.basename(info.name, info.ext)). Do NOT remove --restrict-filenames (AC37). (2) normalizeScanRoot(p) = fs.realpathSync(p) with path.resolve(p) FALLBACK on ANY error (a missing/unmounted root MUST keep a string so it still lands in missingRoots and the E1 mount-loss guard fires -- NEVER drop it). Apply per-root to the merged [...db.folders, ...extraScanRoots] BEFORE the Set dedup at server.js:955. At POST /api/config write (~line 1262) push path.resolve(trimmed) instead of trimmed (AC40); folderSettings keys follow resolved paths. (3) Display-only synthetic folder: GET /api/config derives (never persists) the extraScanRoots download root into the response folders array + a default 'Downloads' name (AC42/43); disabled => extraScanRoots [] => absent (AC4/46). POST /api/config allows a folderSettings entry keyed by the synthetic root while db.folders=validFolders stays synthetic-free (rename persists via folderSettings only, AC44); self-heals since folders re-derive from extraScanRoots each GET (AC45); keep migrateStaleDownloadDirFromFolders. INVARIANT (AC47): the scan/prune path at line 955 reads extraScanRoots directly, NEVER the synthetic GET/config presence -- E1 OR-gate untouched. Unit + integration tests: F regressions (AC33 clean, AC34 My_Home_Movie untouched, AC35 [notanid] untouched, AC36 getMediaId identical); G collapse (AC38), distinct-not-collapsed (AC39), resolve-on-write (AC40), e2e one-entry (AC41), synthetic-not-in-db.folders (AC42), rename-via-folderSettings (AC44), self-heal (AC45), disabled-absent (AC46), prune-independent-of-synthetic/mount-loss (AC47). FLAGGED for the two-reviewer gate (folder-merge + realpath hardening on core always-on code).
tasks.done_when: cleanDisplayTitle cleans a yt-dlp name (AC33) and leaves My_Home_Movie (AC34) + Something [notanid] (AC35) UNCHANGED with getMediaId identical before/after (AC36) and --restrict-filenames intact (AC37); normalizeScanRoot collapses divergent spellings of the SAME tree to one root (AC38) but keeps DISTINCT trees distinct (AC39), a missing/unmounted root still lands in missingRoots (E1 guard fires); db.folders resolved on write (AC40) yields one e2e entry (AC41); GET /api/config surfaces the synthetic folder WITHOUT db.folders (AC42), renamable via folderSettings only (AC44), self-healing (AC45), absent when disabled (AC4/46), and no scan/prune path depends on it (AC47). Lint 0; full suite green; Node 22. Then the two-reviewer gate.

tasks.title: UI: format/quality dropdowns, maxVideos input, pause toggle + edit form, one-shot form, live status polling (2.5s), one-shot rows
tasks.description: Per exec-plan ## Design (FR-B, FR-D UI, FR-A UI, FR-E client). Kept OUT of public/ (served via the conditional registerRoutes gate). subscriptions.html: replace the free-text quality input with a <select> (values best[default],2160p,1440p,1080p,720p,480p,360p = args.QUALITY_ALLOWLIST) + a format <select> (video[default],audio) + a maxVideos numeric input; add the one-shot form (paste-URL + the same two dropdowns); add a per-row pause/resume toggle + an edit form reusing the add fields. subscriptions.js: send quality/format/maxVideos from the selects to POST/PATCH; poll GET /api/subscriptions/status every 2500ms (setInterval cleared on page hide) and merge each entry into its subscription row or render a transient one-shot row (pending -> downloading % -> finished/error, MeTube shape). SECURITY (carry the T5-of-v1.11.0 lesson): every server/user-derived string (name, status title, one-shot label) rendered via textContent / proper escaping, NEVER innerHTML -- no XSS. Pure DOM-helper unit tests where feasible; the rest are the [MANUAL] ACs (13,14,15,26,29,43).
tasks.done_when: Both forms present format+quality dropdowns (AC14/15), one-shot form submits + acks (AC13), maxVideos input wired, per-row pause/resume + edit form present (AC26), status polling every ~2.5s updates rows live pending->downloading%->finished/error (AC29), download folder shows in the sidebar without a manual add (AC43); all attacker-influenceable strings render as TEXT not innerHTML. Disabled => page/nav absent (unchanged from v1.11.0 AC3). Lint 0; full suite green; Node 22. QA-agent review after build-verify given the XSS surface (not the full two-reviewer gate).

tasks.title: FR-I deleted-stays-gone assertion + docs note
tasks.description: Per exec-plan ## Design (FR-I) -- NO production code change. Add a regression/assertion integration test: delete a downloaded video via FileTube's normal delete path, trigger the next poll/re-pull, assert it is NOT re-downloaded (the .ytdlp-archive.txt entry survives delete + prune-missing) (AC52). Add a docs note to README and/or docs/ARCHITECTURE.md on the archive-persistence dependency for network-share download dirs: if the share/archive file is transiently unavailable at poll time, dedup is lost for that poll and the channel may re-download (AC53). Straight-to-SDE, no gate.
tasks.done_when: An assertion test proves delete + prune-missing does not re-download an archived video (AC52); a docs note documents the archive-persistence dependency for network-share dirs (AC53). Lint 0; full suite green; Node 22.

carry_forward_context: tech-debt #10 (docs/exec-plans/tech-debt-tracker.md): the deep prune/mount-loss redesign (Option C -- treat 'a root's entire content vanished at once' as a global unmount signature). Accepted 'ship now, track'; NOT in scope for v1.12.0 unless PE finds G's realpath work naturally subsumes part of it (flag if so; do NOT expand scope silently).

The prior v1.11.0 feature record lives in docs/exec-plans/completed/2026-07-05-yt-dlp-integration-module.md; locked decisions D1-D5 (exactly 5 ENV vars, cookies-file-only, dedicated /subscriptions page, pinned yt-dlp, no runtime auto-update) still hold and constrain this feature. FILETUBE_YTDLP_MAX_VIDEOS is an EXISTING global (default 25) that workstream C overrides per-channel.
product_forks_for_dean: None expected. Dean has LOCKED: one feature; polling (no WebSocket); dropdowns; display-only synthetic folder (softening C7(ii) is approved); per-channel N override of the global default; embed metadata for BOTH audio and video; one-shot single-video-only into 'One-Off' + 202 background. If the PM surfaces a GENUINE new product decision not covered here, flag it to Dean before Design; otherwise proceed on these locks.

history.note: Feature initiated by Dean: v1.12.0 mini-MeTube parity for the optional yt-dlp module. ONE cohesive feature (9 workstreams A-I), MeTube-simple shape, poll-based status (no WebSocket). Absorbs the parked feature/ytdlp-oneshot-download as workstream A. Bug fixes F/G/I have CONFIRMED mechanics with file:line evidence -- baked in so PM/PE don't re-investigate. Read the shipped v1.11.0 state + locked decisions D1-D5, CONTRIBUTING.md (Node 22), RELIABILITY.md (spawn try/catch, keep binaries out of the core suite).

EM verified the F/G bug-fix line references against current server.js (:511 md5 path-id, :955 byte-identical Set-dedup, :998 basename title -- all confirmed). Reset state from the completed v1.11.0 feature to this new v1.12.0 feature (prior record preserved in docs/exec-plans/completed/). Wrote the Discovery inbox for product-manager at .state/inbox/product-manager.md turning the 9 workstreams into grouped ACs (F/G/I as concrete pass/fail ACs, not open investigations) with the disabled-no-op north star + the F/G core-scan exception scoped. Routed to PM. Awaiting PM output, then /prep-pe-design.
